steps.title: Write the length
steps.content: The width is $w$. The length is $38$ centimeters greater than the width, so the length is $w+38$ centimeters.

steps.title: Use rectangle area
steps.content:
The area of a rectangle is length times width. Using width $w$ and length $w+38$ gives:
$$A=(w)(w+38)$$

steps.title: Match the choices
steps.content: Choice A uses $\frac{w}{38}$ for the length, which makes the length a fraction of the width. Choice B uses $38w$, which means $38$ times the width. Choice D uses $w^2$, which would make the block a square. The expression $A=(w)(w+38)$ matches the stated length. The answer is C.
